signals.headline: Key developments:**
signals.raw:
Key developments:**

South Korea's annual inflation rose to **2.2% in March 2026**, exceeding the BOK's 2% target and earlier forecasts of 2.1%[1]. This acceleration follows a period of moderation when inflation held at 2% in both January and February[1].

signals.headline: Bank of Korea Holds Rates Steady Amid Rising Oil-Driven Inflation** | NO | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea Holds Rates Steady Amid Rising Oil-Driven Inflation** | NO | HIGH confidence

The Bank of Korea kept its benchmark interest rate at 2.5% for a seventh consecutive month on April 10, 2026, following its Monetary Policy Board's unanimous decision[3]. While this decision maintains the status quo rather than announcing a policy shift, it comes with a significant upward revision: the cent

signals.headline: Market implications:**
signals.raw:
Market implications:**

The BOK's decision to hold rates steady while signaling higher inflation expectations reflects policymakers' focus on external risks rather than cutting rates despite moderating growth prospects[2]. The Policy Board expects 2026 economic growth to fall below 2% as currency weakness (with the won trading around 1,520 per dollar) compounds inflation concerns[3].
